changes.before: "I was never going to be enough alone," she said. "Not at the border. Not in Lordaeron. Not in the throne room. We stopped him because we were right together. Not because I was right and you were useful."
changes.after: "I was never going to be enough alone at the border, in Lordaeron, or in the throne room," she said. "We stopped him because we were right together, with both of us doing the work."

changes.before: "I was never going to be enough alone," she said. "Not at the border. Not in Lordaeron. Not in the throne room. We stopped him because we were right together. Not because I was right and you were useful."
changes.after: "I was never going to be enough alone at the border, in Lordaeron, or in the throne room," she said. "We stopped him because we were right together, with both of us doing the work."

changes.before:
But the thing in Sylvanas's chest was not Frostmourne.

Kalar'shorel'anor.
changes.after: But what hit Sylvanas in the chest was Kalar'shorel'anor.

changes.before: Arthas had gone the same way by a different road. Certainty. Momentum. No one left beside him with enough standing to say stop. Uther gone. Jaina gone. The next step always close enough to excuse the one after it.
changes.after: Arthas had gone the same way by a different road. Certainty. Momentum. No one left beside him with enough standing to say stop. Uther gone. Jaina gone. They kept using the next step to justify the one before it.

changes.before: The eyes traveled over her face with scholarly patience.
changes.after: It studied her face with scholarly patience.

changes.before: "I wanted you to understand the size of what you touched." Its gaze lifted slightly, toward the vault above. "You killed the hand holding the knife. You did not break the arm."
changes.after: "I wanted you to understand the size of what you touched." Its head angled slightly toward the vault above. "You killed the wielder. The power behind him is still here."

changes.before: The words sat between them.
changes.after: Neither of them spoke.

changes.before: Jaina had said the same thing through the wall, and hearing it here—hearing it from the mouth of a thing that spoke of Frostmourne as its craft—gave the truth a deeper chill.
changes.after: Jaina had said the same thing through the wall, and hearing it here—from the mouth of a thing that spoke of Frostmourne as its craft—chilled her more deeply.

changes.before: Recognition flickered across its face.
changes.after: It showed recognition.

changes.before: "No. Boasting is for victories that need witnesses." The green-gold eyes held hers. "I came because you had earned a look at the hand behind the work."
changes.after: "No. Boasting is for victories that need witnesses." It kept its attention on her. "I came because you had earned a look at the hand behind the work."

changes.before: No answer to that yet. The better question came at once.
changes.after: No answer to that yet. Sylvanas thought of the better question at once.

changes.before: The faces would change. The body would change. The voice would change. Those were tools. But the farewell had survived five masks and a prison visit. That meant it sat deeper than discipline. Older than this campaign. Older, perhaps, than the world as it stood now.
changes.after: The faces would change. The body would change. The voice would change. Those were tools. But the farewell had survived five masks and a prison visit. That meant the habit behind it belonged to something older than discipline. Older than this campaign. Older, perhaps, than the world as it stood now.

changes.before: A network could listen for words.
changes.after: Farstriders, quartermasters, runners, and archive mages could listen for the phrase.

changes.before: The eyes traveled over her face with scholarly patience.
changes.after: It studied her face with scholarly patience.

changes.before: "I wanted you to understand the size of what you touched." Its gaze lifted slightly, toward the vault above. "You killed the hand holding the knife. You did not break the arm."
changes.after: "I wanted you to understand the size of what you touched." Its head angled slightly toward the vault above. "You killed the wielder. The power behind him is still here."

changes.before: She could hear Sylvanas listening. No interruption. No mercy either.
changes.after: Sylvanas listened. No interruption. No mercy either.

changes.before: Knowing had not been enough.
changes.after: She had still failed with it in her hands.

changes.before: The prison had taken sight, touch, movement, any honest route between them. It had not taken the work.
changes.after: The prison had taken sight, touch, movement, any honest route between them. They could still pass the blade between them.

changes.before: Two men certain they could carry their own brilliance without help.
changes.after: Two men sure they could do it alone.

changes.before: It was tall enough to make the cell feel smaller. Horns or ridges brushed the shadow near the ceiling. Wings folded close along a frame built for wider places than this. The proportions were wrong in ways no armor could explain. They belonged to a creature for whom doors were a courtesy offered by lesser beings.
changes.after: It was tall enough to make the cell feel smaller. Horns or ridges brushed the shadow near the ceiling. Wings folded close along a frame built for wider places than this. The proportions were wrong in ways no armor could explain. They belonged to something that treated doors like they did not apply to it.

changes.before: The words sat between them.
changes.after: Neither of them spoke.

changes.before: "Devotion," Jaina said. "The kind with an empty place in the middle."
changes.after: "Devotion," Jaina said. "The kind that wants someone else to tell it what to do."

changes.before: "Millhaven," Jaina went on. "Andorhal. Scholomance. Northrend. I kept telling myself I was the one carrying the answer. Even after it had stopped being true."
changes.after: "Millhaven," Jaina went on. "Andorhal. Scholomance. Northrend. I kept telling myself I was the one who knew what came next. Even after that stopped being true."

changes.before: "Yes." No heat in the answer. "A nuisance. Not a ruin."
changes.after: "Yes." No heat in the answer.

changes.before: "I wanted you to understand the size of what you touched." Its gaze lifted slightly, toward the vault above. "You killed the hand holding the knife. You did not break the arm."
changes.after: "I wanted you to understand the size of what you touched." Its head angled slightly toward the vault above. "You killed the wielder. The power behind him is still here."

changes.before: "Devotion," Jaina said. "The kind with an empty place in the middle."
changes.after: "Devotion," Jaina said. "The kind that wants someone else to tell it what to do."

changes.before: "How much plague, how much fear, how much order built from death before he broke." The voice stayed calm. "Quite a lot, as it happens."
changes.after: "How much plague could spread, how much fear it could buy, how many people death would force into line before he broke." The voice stayed calm. "Quite a lot, as it happens."
